user_feedback: Generated TT preset is basically bang on when played alongside the original tone.

tone_profile.input_summary: AC/DC Jailbreak Malcolm Young dry studio rhythm crunch

classification_reasoning.style_reasoning: AC/DC and Jailbreak imply classic 1970s British hard rock with no modern metal elements.
classification_reasoning.era_reasoning: 1970s analogue recording practices: dry, raw rhythm guitar driven by tube amp crunch.
classification_reasoning.role_reasoning: Malcolm Young is strongly associated with tight, dry rhythm guitar.
classification_reasoning.gain_reasoning: Low-to-medium crunch preserves chord separation and pick attack.
classification_reasoning.effects_reasoning: The target tone is dry and direct, so delays, reverbs, modulation and rack effects should be avoided by default.
classification_reasoning.family_reasoning: Vintage Marshall-style British crunch is the correct family. In TT testing, Brit 8000 with low PreAmp and high Master produced a very close result.

learning_notes: This profile was validated by playing the generated TT preset alongside the Spotify reference track.
Brit 8000 with low PreAmp and high Master works well as a Malcolm Young-style rhythm platform.
Brit Green / Greenback-style speakers are highly suitable for early AC/DC rhythm.
Dynamic 57 provides attack and upper-mid bite.
Ribbon 121 adds body and smooths the top end.
No rack EQ, boost, delay, reverb or modulation should be used by default.
Avoid choosing amp models because they contain words like vintage, metal or lead unless the full tone profile supports that choice.
negative_constraints: Do not select Vintage Metal Lead for this tone.
Do not select Metal Lead V for this tone.
Do not select Modern Tube Lead for this tone.
Do not select Red Pig for this tone unless specifically requested.
Do not select Rectifier-style or ENGL-style amps for this tone unless specifically requested.
Do not add rack EQ by default.
Do not add boost pedals by default.
Do not add delay, reverb or modulation by default.
